name: Drip coffee makers
summary: Automatic drip coffee makers, $50-$500 range. Includes SCA-certified, non-certified, and pod systems.

evidence.claim: SCA (Specialty Coffee Association) Home Brewer Program certifies brew temperature 197.6-204.8°F, total contact time 4-8 minutes, and uniform water distribution across the coffee bed.
'12-cup' carafes use 5 oz 'cups' not 8 oz — a 12-cup carafe holds 60 oz, or roughly 7.5 US standard cups.
Pod systems (Keurig, Nespresso) have significantly higher per-cup operating cost than drip or espresso machines with whole-bean input; annual pod cost commonly exceeds the machine's purchase price.

criteria.name: brew_temperature_f
criteria.notes: SCA standard is 197.6-204.8°F (E1). Below 195°F is under-extracted; above 210°F is scalded.

criteria.name: brew_time_minutes
criteria.notes: SCA standard is 4-8 minutes total contact time (E1).

criteria.name: capacity_cups
criteria.notes: Industry 'cups' are 5 oz, not 8 oz (E2). Convert to actual ounces for serving-size comparison.

criteria.name: sca_certified
criteria.notes: SCA Home Brewer certification is the most rigorous consumer-facing quality mark.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Industry 'cup' is 5 oz; a 12-cup carafe holds ~7.5 US standard 8-oz cups (E2). Users buying for two people rarely need more than an 8-industry-cup model.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the 'cup' unit specified? If not, assume 5 oz industry-standard.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Quality depends far more on the bean freshness, grind, and water quality than on the drip machine (within SCA-certified models). Non-SCA 'gourmet' claims are marketing.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the machine hold SCA Home Brewer certification? If not, 'gourmet' is unverifiable.

confabulationPatterns.reality: For pod systems (Keurig, Nespresso), annual pod costs frequently exceed the machine's purchase price (E3). Drip or French press with whole beans is 60-80% cheaper per cup.

compatibilityQuestions: Do you want whole-bean input (drip + grinder), pre-ground (drip only), or pod? Each has very different annual costs.

repairability.notes: Technivorm, Ratio, and Breville have decent parts markets. Most Cuisinart, Hamilton Beach, and Mr. Coffee models are not practically repairable.
